speaker: DOCTOR
text: Hi, Ms. Smith. What brings you in today?

speaker: PATIENT
text: I've got a really bad headache. I've never had one like this.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: When did it start?

speaker: PATIENT
text: About two hours ago. It hit me all at once, like something exploded in my head.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Where is the pain?

speaker: PATIENT
text: All over, but mostly the back of my head and my neck feels stiff.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: On a scale of one to ten?

speaker: PATIENT
text: A ten. It's the worst headache of my life.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Any nausea, vomiting, or vision changes?

speaker: PATIENT
text: I feel sick to my stomach, and bright light really bothers me.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Any weakness, numbness, or trouble speaking?

speaker: PATIENT
text: No, nothing like that.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: You said this started two hours ago — was it sudden or gradual?

speaker: PATIENT
text: Actually, it's been closer to an hour. I was trying to wait it out before coming in.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Okay. Any history of migraines?

speaker: PATIENT
text: I get headaches sometimes, but they're never like this. This is different.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Any medications?

speaker: PATIENT
text: I took two acetaminophen about thirty minutes ago. No relief. I'm also on sumatriptan for my usual migraines, but I haven't taken it today.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Any family history of aneurysm or stroke?

speaker: PATIENT
text: My aunt had a brain bleed in her fifties.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: I'm going to examine your neck and do a neurologic exam now.
